div.head: The Minister in Haiti (Armour) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 15. Department’s telegram No. 9, February 24, 10 a.m., regarding the Franco-Haitian commercial agreement.Signed March 10, 1934, Le Moniteur, April 23, 1934, p. 255. The agreement being a true “avenant”, the commercial convention of April 12, 1930 between France and HaitiLe Moniteur, August 7, 1930, p. 245. continues in effect except where it is in conflict with the terms of the “avenant”. Furthermore, the fact that the French products benefiting by the terms of the convention are definitely specified by name would seem to preclude like treatment for similar American products. However, as Mr. De la Rue will be in Washington about March 1, the Department may wish to discuss this matter with him.
